name: LazyData's Kill Skill Variant
description: Vex endgame setup that keeps kill skills active without action-skill micromanagement.
whyThisWorks: Kill skills remain active through combat flow without strict action timing.
Patch 1.030 critical hit fixes improve consistency at UVH6.
Simple execution keeps DPS stable under pressure.
strategicContext: This is the most broadly adopted Vex progression-to-endgame bridge because it minimizes execution tax while preserving top-tier scaling.
advancedTactics: Prioritize crit windows over overextending for one extra kill chain.
Refresh kill-skill stacks before arena transition waves.

name: MANTRARMY Gun Specialist
description: Weapon-agnostic Vex setup tuned for true UVH6 adaptability.
whyThisWorks: Scales with weapon archetypes instead of one required drop.
Remains UVH6 viable under imperfect loot conditions.
Low lock-in makes iterative optimization practical.
strategicContext: The post-1.030 weapon pool rewards builds that adapt to strong rolls quickly. This setup keeps that flexibility without sacrificing endgame floor.
whenToAvoid: Dedicated speed-clear records with fixed loadout
advancedTactics: Re-tune one passive node each time your core weapon archetype changes.
Use shield uptime as your damage tempo indicator.

name: Ricochet Cannon Endgame
description: Rafa ricochet core build with top-tier mob clear and high consistency.
whyThisWorks: Ricochet chains transform mob density into bonus damage throughput.
Cannons stabilize single-target pressure while ricochet handles packs.
Patch 1.030 made ricochet interactions more reliable.
strategicContext: Rafa excels when ricochet uptime is maximized and cannon cadence covers elite windows. This build is the current reference implementation.
advancedTactics: Maintain Lateral Plinking before entering new density pockets.
Angle opening shots off geometry for immediate multi-hit chains.

name: Splash and Dash
description: High mobility Rafa variant mixing splash bursts with route tempo control.
whyThisWorks: High movement uptime reduces incoming damage and clears routes faster.
Splash package smooths inconsistent aim moments in farm loops.
Flexible weapons allow quick adaptation to map-specific spawns.
strategicContext: Farm efficiency is mostly movement and reset speed; this build optimizes both while preserving safe clear thresholds.
advancedTactics: Chain dash windows with reload cancels between combat pockets.
Use splash pre-fire around corners to maintain route momentum.
performanceNotes: Strong route velocity with moderate boss ceiling.
optimizationTips: Maintain movement buff uptime between packs.

name: HORBA - Heavy Ordnance Ricochet Bananza
description: AncientRune's flagship Amon endgame build built around heavy ordnance sustain and double-dipping DOT scaling.
whyThisWorks: Primal Surge plus Super Soldier creates near-infinite heavy-ordnance uptime.
Tail of the Comet and Blackout double-dip status scaling for massive DOT output.
Scourge and Firewall keep survivability and multiplicative damage online.
strategicContext: HORBA is the benchmark Amon package for true UVH6 because it combines sustainable ammo economy with top-end scaling interactions.
advancedTactics: Trigger status stacks before ordnance burst windows for peak double-dip value.
Switch to Onslaught variant for mobbing maps with long travel segments.

name: Nuclear Winter Harlowe
description: Flux Generator battlefield-control build with high support value and strong DOT layering.
whyThisWorks: Flux field gives control, ally shielding, and sustained DOT pressure.
Group Study stacking converts uptime into escalating gun damage.
Teleport capstone allows tactical field relocation and revives.
strategicContext: Harlowe's strongest identity in 1.030 is persistent battlefield advantage rather than burst. This build maximizes that identity.
whenToAvoid: Pure speedruns where setup time is costly
advancedTactics: Maintain 4-6 Group Study stacks before elite spawn triggers.
Relocate field proactively to next objective lane.

name: Kinetic Crit Phamiliar Vex
description: Top leveling Vex progression path leveraging companion pressure and critical scaling.
whyThisWorks: Companion handling reduces mechanical load for fast progression.
Crit scaling transitions cleanly into endgame-focused Vex paths.
Low gear dependency keeps pace consistent from 1-65.
strategicContext: This is the safest Vex leveling route for players who want smooth pathing into competitive endgame builds.
advancedTactics: Keep companion in frontline while you angle crit farming lanes.
Prioritize survivability nodes until mid-campaign gear stabilizes.

name: CHROMA Accelerator Harlowe
description: Leveling-focused Harlowe route centered on cryo AOE and simple mid-range control.
whyThisWorks: CHROMA AOE clears leveling packs with low execution complexity.
Cryo slows reduce incoming pressure during weak-gear phases.
Simple action loop makes campaign pacing efficient.
strategicContext: For new Harlowe progression, action-skill consistency matters more than theoretical DPS peaks.
advancedTactics: Drop CHROMA at choke entries and pull groups through it.
Reserve cooldown for elite waves rather than single targets.

name: Heavy Ordnance Leveling Amon
description: Higher-skill Amon leveling route that sets up direct transition into HORBA-style endgame.
whyThisWorks: Progressive ordnance scaling builds foundation for endgame Amon archetypes.
Ammo sustain skills reduce hard stalls in long leveling routes.
High damage rewards positioning discipline early.
strategicContext: Not the easiest leveling route, but best for players committed to high-end Amon ordnance play later.
advancedTactics: Use terrain to offset self-damage risk while leveling launchers.
Prioritize ammo economy nodes before pure damage nodes.

name: Bleed Witch VEX (Moxsy)
description: Moxsy's flagship VEX bleed scaler adapted for post-nerf boss deletion and UVH6 clears.
whyThisWorks: Bleed amplification front-loads boss health bars when stacks are pre-ramped.
Dead Ringer cycling creates repeatable burst windows instead of one risky all-in.
Post-nerf tuning keeps kill-time competitive without crit-knife dependency.
strategicContext: Still one of the strongest VEX boss-kill templates when piloted correctly; community considers it high-ceiling but patch-resilient after adaptation.
whenToAvoid: Beginner progression runs with low gear stability
advancedTactics: Pre-stack bleed on adds before swapping to boss burst cycle.
Time Dead Ringer exits with curse proc windows for peak burst.
optimizationTips: Prioritize status-effect scaling rolls on artifact/class mod.
Maintain 2-weapon rotation so bleed uptime never drops.
performanceNotes: Documented one-shot style boss sequences in optimized windows.
Top-tier single target; moderate execution tax.

name: Curse Bleed VEX (Moxsy)
description: Post-Halloween patch VEX adaptation that preserved top-tier bleed scaling with safer execution.
whyThisWorks: Curse interactions maintain high DPS after crit-knife exploit nerfs.
Lower mechanical burden than full one-shot variants.
Scales well in UVH6 mixed packs and boss transitions.
strategicContext: Community-standard post-nerf VEX setup: less explosive than launch Bleed Witch, but far more stable across patches and encounters.
advancedTactics: Refresh curse stacks before elite spawns, not after.
Use add packs to rebuild tempo before boss re-engage.
optimizationTips: Favor cooldown and curse-duration stats over raw crit chance.
Run one flexible weapon slot for map-specific resistance checks.
performanceNotes: High meta durability after exploit nerf wave.

name: No Crit Knife VEX (Moxsy)
description: Intricate VEX build designed to remove crit-knife exploit reliance while retaining UVH6 output.
whyThisWorks: Avoids exploit dependency that developers have targeted repeatedly.
Keeps competitive UVH6 damage through systemic scaling paths.
More future-proof for ongoing balance changes.
strategicContext: Moxsy's long-term stability pick for VEX players who want high output without betting on exploitable mechanics surviving patches.
whenToAvoid: New players uncomfortable with intricate rotations
advancedTactics: Treat action-skill timing as reset logic, not raw burst button.
Practice stack maintenance on test arenas before boss runs.
optimizationTips: Track patch notes for indirect status-scaling changes.
Use practice loops to reduce rotation drop-offs.
performanceNotes: Lower patch-risk profile than exploit-centered alternatives.

name: Rafa Peacebreaker Sniper (Moxsy)
description: UVH6 Rafa sniper build centered on Peacebreaker Cannons and precision burst sequencing.
whyThisWorks: Extremely high weak-point burst for UVH6 bosses.
Cannons cover movement windows where sniper uptime drops.
Great fit for teams needing dedicated elite delete role.
strategicContext: Core Moxsy Rafa endgame recommendation for players comfortable with precision aiming and target-priority discipline.
whenToUse: Co-op teams with established frontline control
whenToAvoid: High-chaos mobbing where sight lines are unstable
advancedTactics: Pre-angle cannon lanes before sniper commitment.
Rotate to sidearm during forced movement to maintain DPS continuity.
optimizationTips: Stack weak-point and handling stats over reload speed.
Anchor around cover to reduce aim disruption.
performanceNotes: Can spike higher with perfect weak-point loops.

name: Rafa Slash n Dash Melee (Moxsy)
description: Arc-Knives melee Rafa build with full green-tree tempo for UVH6 close-range clears.
whyThisWorks: Delivers top-tier tempo in dense maps with short travel distances.
Green-tree mobility keeps uptime high despite melee risk.
UVH6 viable when survivability layers are maintained.
strategicContext: Moxsy's melee Rafa path trades range safety for map-control speed and high engagement tempo in late-game loops.
whenToAvoid: Long-range boss arenas with poor melee windows
advancedTactics: Use dash chains to maintain invulnerability frames between packs.
Open with ranged setup tag before full melee commit.
optimizationTips: Prioritize shield gate and melee amp synergy.
Route pulls so you never engage isolated high-risk targets first.
performanceNotes: Outstanding clear speed; higher failure rate without defensive discipline.

name: Harlowe CHROMA Accelerator (Moxsy)
description: CHROMA-gravitar burst Harlowe setup tuned for elite deletes and flexible weapon usage.
whyThisWorks: Strong burst windows with broad weapon compatibility.
Group Study style scaling keeps damage relevant through UVH6 fights.
Flexible play pattern supports both solo and co-op progression.
strategicContext: Moxsy highlights this as a practical Harlowe bridge between high-end burst and real-world gear flexibility.
whenToAvoid: Pure support-focused team compositions needing utility-first play
advancedTactics: Prime CHROMA field before elite spawn triggers.
Use burst windows for priority targets, then reset with utility fire.
optimizationTips: Favor elemental uptime stats to stabilize damage floor.
Don't overhold action skill; cycle for tempo.
performanceNotes: Popular for “use almost any gun” flexibility without collapse in UVH6.

name: Harlowe Radiation Farm Loop (Moxsy)
description: Radiation-focused Harlowe setup built for repeatable boss farming and add-wave melt.
whyThisWorks: Radiation chains improve clear speed in farm loops with dense add packs.
Flux field provides steady survivability during repetitive boss attempts.
Low execution burden supports long farming sessions.
strategicContext: A consistency-first farming build with strong sustain and route tempo, designed around repeated runs rather than single kill showcases.
whenToAvoid: Short sprint clears where setup time is too costly
advancedTactics: Drag add packs through radiation fields before boss commit.
Reset field position between phases to protect revival lanes.
optimizationTips: Prioritize radiation proc chance and cooldown reduction.
Use route-specific weapon swaps to avoid resistance stalls.
performanceNotes: Excellent long-session consistency and low downtime.
